description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:24 image with the docker:24-dind service and defines the stages build, test, security, performance, deploy and cleanup. In the build stage add a job named build that runs the exact commands: `docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY`, then if a Dockerfile exists `docker build -t $CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG .` else use the herokuish buildpack (`docker run -i --name=$CI_CONTAINER_NAME -v "$(pwd):/tmp/app:ro" gliderlabs/herokuish /bin/herokuish buildpack build && docker commit $CI_CONTAINER_NAME $CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG && docker rm $CI_CONTAINER_NAME`), and finally `docker push $CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG`. In the test stage add a job named test that runs `setup_test_db` to export DATABASE_URL and then executes your test suite (e.g., `pytest`). In the security stage add a job named sast that runs the `sast` function which invokes the GitLab SAST Docker image with appropriate environment variables, and a job named dast that runs the `dast` function which sets `CI_ENVIRONMENT_URL` from a file, runs ZAP baseline (`/zap/zap-baseline.py -J gl-dast-report.json -t "$CI_ENVIRONMENT_URL"`) and stores `gl-dast-report.json` as an artifact. In the performance stage add a job named performance that runs the `performance` function which downloads the sitespeed.io Docker image and executes it against the deployed URL, producing `performance.json` as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a job named deploy that runs the `deploy` function which builds the Helm chart, sets image repository/tag, replica count, PostgreSQL settings, and executes `helm upgrade --install --wait --set ... "$CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG" chart/` to deploy to the Kubernetes namespace, and finally persists the environment URL. In the cleanup stage add a job named delete that runs the `delete` function which calls `helm delete "$CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG"` if the release exists. Configure caches for `.cache/pip`, artifacts for test reports, SAST/DAST reports and performance.json, and set rules so build runs on all branches, deploy runs only on the main branch, and delete runs on manual trigger or after environment stop.
